key_insight: An open-source Firedrake-based finite-element framework, "H-cloud," makes superconducting electromagnetic simulations transparent, cloud-accessible, and ready for optimization workflows.
engineering_relevance: It enables reproducible high-fidelity modeling of applied superconductors on local machines or in Google Colab without changing the formulation, lowering the barrier to benchmarking and design studies.
method: Curl-conforming Nédélec FEM for nonlinear magneto-quasistatic superconducting response
Explicit variational treatment of tangential field boundary conditions, E-J power law, and fully implicit time stepping
Implemented in Python with Firedrake/UFL and PETSc nonlinear solvers for portable cloud execution
evidence_signal: The framework matched an independently built COMSOL benchmark for a Bi2212 wire and reproduced magnetization loops to about 1% with peak errors below 1.5% on matched meshes.
watch_item: Cloud execution preserves the solution, but reduced hardware in browser environments can add runtime cost and may limit large-scale studies.
paper_title: A Cloud-Accessible Open-Source Framework for the Electromagnetic Modelling of Applied Superconductors
